text: By the time I arrived, she had left.
She opened a new shop after she had sold the old one.
I had been dreaming of change for years before I left.
When the fire happened, she had already decided to start over.
Pricila, can you tell me about a defining moment in your life?
Of course. By the time I turned fifty, I had been running my clinic for nearly twenty years, and I had a real setback, my health forced me to slow down.
That must have felt daunting. How did you overcome it?
In hindsight, it was a pivotal moment. I had been dreaming of travel for years, so I took a leap of faith and embarked on a new chapter.
And looking back now, do you have any regrets?
None at all. Against all odds, I put the old life behind me, and this chapter has been the most rewarding of them all.

Looking back, I can see now that everything changed in one single year. By the time I turned forty-five, I had been running my clinic for almost two decades, and I had convinced myself that my path was already set. Then came the setback. My closest partner had decided to leave, and at first it felt daunting, even frightening. But in hindsight, that was the most pivotal moment of all. I had been dreaming of slowing down for years, and suddenly the door had opened. So I took a leap of faith. I put the old plan behind me, and I embarked on a completely new chapter -- one that has turned out to be far more rewarding than I ever imagined.

People often ask me what the secret was. Honestly, there was no secret. By the time the big decision came, I had already failed at two other things, and I had learned more from those failures than from any success. Against all odds, those setbacks paved the way. They taught me to trust a gut feeling, to put the past behind me, and to keep moving forward. So when the pivotal moment finally arrived, I was ready. I had been waiting for it, in a way, my whole life. And the chapter that followed was, by far, the most defining of them all.
